world.label: NEW ALBION 2040
world.title: The Mooring Ledger
world.blurb: The high field loses its tally-master. The wire-sheets print every rumor in the city, and printed nothing.
tuning: Verne's machinery with le Carré's people: the wonder is routine to them; the fear is not.
Steam and print: every scene smells of coal, oil, ink, or rain on hot iron.
The sky is infrastructure. Nobody looks up unless something is wrong.
Digital newsprint reads as gospel at dusk and kindling by dawn — say so without saying so.
world.victimRole: tally-master of the high mooring field
world.site: the foot of the north mooring mast
world.stashPlace: the deflation locker on the {WORD} berth

ledger.A: Aurora Borealis — broken up for gasbags after the Kettering fire.
ledger.C: Cormorant — sold to the mail service; flies nights, badly.
ledger.D: Daedalus — lost over the Irish Sea with a hold of parlor pianos.
ledger.E: Empress of Leeds — condemned; her frame is the tea pavilion now.
ledger.G: Great Elector — impounded for debts in three currencies.
ledger.I: Iron Swallow — deflated and forgotten behind the customs shed.
ledger.K: Kestrel — burned at her mast; the inquest blamed the weather.
ledger.N: Nimbus Queen — retired to hauling soap adverts over the piers.
ledger.O: Ozymandias — half-built and abandoned when the money learned arithmetic.
ledger.P: Perseverance — traded to a circus, for reasons the registry does not give.
ledger.R: Resolute — dismasted in the gale of '38; never rigged again.
ledger.V: Valkyrie — struck off after the mutiny nobody printed.

herrings.name: SPRAGG
herrings.trade: sells patent boiler salts off a steam velocipede
herrings.clears: was three days in the Weald selling salts, and the tollhouse punch-cards agree

herrings.name: LUMB
herrings.trade: deals in secondhand type and stolen headlines
herrings.clears: sat those nights in the magistrates' cells over a libel

details.phrase: favored the left hand on the mooring winch
details.counter: lost the left glove and two tendons to a fan blade in spring, and has worked gloved-right since; the surgeon's bill is on file

details.phrase: wore a brass respirator badge on the off lapel
details.counter: pawned his badge at Michaelmas; the ticket sits in the pawn book yet

details.phrase: counted the mast stairs aloud going up
details.counter: has not spoken since the Kettering fire, and the whole field knows it

beats: {VICTIM} rides the funicular to the high field, tally book chained at the wrist
{VICTIM} is met at the dark end of the mooring row by a man come down from the offices
One man rides the funicular down. The tally book does not
openingLines: The dirigibles come up the estuary at dusk in slow grey
processions, and the city reads about itself on wire-sheets
that are gospel by dark and kindling by dawn. Steam does the
work in New Albion. Print does the believing.
This week the high mooring field lost its tally-master.
{VICTIM} kept the count of everything that came off the sky,
and was found at the foot of the north mast with the count
unfinished. The wire-sheets, which print every rumor in the
city nightly, printed nothing. Bought silence is the loudest
Four names held the inside of the Trust. Three trails will
bring them down to one: the coin, the keys, and the one
witness nobody ever thought worth buying.
